text: schema. References to the source project's `FLOW/L*.md`, `docs/SPEC.md`, or registers are
- this view is `price_view = raw_usd_view` from the input contract (spec §2)
- One parquet file serves both training and the OOS test — that is why it has no cuts and no features (register C-71).
- We split each ticker's series into three windows (dates = parameters, spec §5a).
- The windows are indices on one continuous series, not separate parquet files (register C-71).
- The detector returns setup objects (contract spec §3).
contract: every fact below is a faithful English translation of the source spec §1–§8
(`docs/SPEC.md`, version 1.2) plus the fixed reference values pinned for this single-source-of-truth

| `closed_through_line` | `1 if sign·(c − L_trend(t)) > 0 else 0` | flag | int {0,1} | break signal. **In Output B (row = `t0`) this is definitionally = 1 → it is an audit/invariant column and is NOT part of `FEATURE_MANIFEST`** (`test_entry_break_invariant`) |
> **Status: "reference design (one valid realization)".** The master contract (`docs/SPEC.md` §3, §9)
- `L_trend`, `L_opp` are least-squares fits over swing indices **all `≤ t0`** (SPEC §3 invariant 4).
(glossary L6; SPEC §4 v1.2 note — the break candle is deliberately included in the normalizer).
| `MIN_TOUCHES` | `2` | swing touches | contract (`config/params.json`, SPEC §7) |
| `TOUCH_TOL` | `0.25` | × `ATR(t)` | reference design (one valid realization); name reserved by SPEC §3; value set here and in `config/params.json` |
| `H` | `24` | candles (horizon) | contract (SPEC §7) |
| `W_ATR` | `14` | candles | contract (SPEC §7) |
| `ATR_VARIANT` | `wilder` | — | contract (SPEC §7) |
| `EPS` (ε) | `1e-9` | — | contract (SPEC §7) |
Dedup rule (SPEC §3): **one swing-touch counts once**, not every adjacent candle that happens to lie
`ATR(t0) > 0`. It is **not** part of any barrier (barriers are geometric; SPEC §3/§5 — "no ATR↔label
**Which swings define `L_trend` (per SPEC §3: "fit through the touchpoints").**
**missing** → the setup is **rejected and counted** as DET-09 `missing_L_opp` (§8). SPEC §3 invariant 3
breaks the line in the trade direction (SPEC §3 invariant 2, close-based):
## 8. DET-09 — rejection + audit (SPEC §3 invariant 5)
## 11. Contract conformance — mapping to SPEC §3
The upstream `docs/SPEC.md`, `FLOW/`, and `docs/PIPELINE_REVIEW_CONFIGURABLES.md` are provenance only.
- **Asset** — any instrument with OHLCV data; the pipeline is independent of price scale and timeframe (spec §1).
- **TF (timeframe)** — candle resolution; default `1h` (parameter spec §7).

- **Parameters** — the only place for configuration = `config/params.json` (contract spec §7); zero thresholds hardcoded in the code. The most important: `TF`, `H=24`, `MIN_TOUCHES=2`, `W_ATR=14`, `W_VOL=20`, `ATR_VARIANT=wilder`, `PRICE_VIEW=raw_usd_view`, `THRESHOLD_ENTRY=0.60`, `PURGE_CANDLES=H`, `EMBARGO_SESSIONS=5
- **volume required** — for this universe, missing volume = hard fail QC (spec §2).
- **ET-naive → UTC tz-aware conversion** — done only by the F1 reader at the read boundary (spec §2); in the archive the time stays naive.
- **`price_view = raw_usd_view`** — the semantic name of the input price view from the contract (spec §2/§7); one value per dataset (manifest), guarantees a repeatable result.
- **NaN/Inf** — 0 in Output B apart from documented cases (spec §8).

| Presentation language | viz UI + `README.md` = English; source project's `FLOW/`, `ROADMAP.md`, `docs/SPEC.md`, register = Polish; this English glossary + the `ENG/` summaries = English (snapshot) | — |

> **Threshold provenance.** The master contract (`docs/SPEC.md`, v1.2) defers the *numeric thresholds* of L8 to roadmap item **F4b**. Every threshold and every invented structural choice in this document is therefore labelled **reference design (one valid realization)**. The *set* of counters/parities, the FAIL-on-any-
| `nan_inf_outputB` | NaN/Inf values in Output B beyond the **documented** exceptions of `docs/SPEC.md` §8 | SPEC §8, DoD |

> **Documented Output-B exceptions (do NOT count toward `nan_inf_outputB`).** Per `docs/SPEC.md` §8 the only sanctioned non-finite is `volume_z_score = NaN` when an asset has no volume **and** carries the explicit volume-missing flag. For the S&P 500 universe, volume is *required* (missing volume is itself a hard QC fa

All thresholds below are **reference design (one valid realization)** — `docs/SPEC.md` defers the numbers to F4b. They are fixed here so the gate is buildable today; F4b may re-pin them in `config/params.json` (the only configuration site), but the *comparison operators and the structure are frozen*.

**Pipeline B L4 reference (v2, textbook + guards):** `RSI_n` (Wilder), `MACD(12,26,9)`, `ATR_n` (Wilder), `OBV`, `ADL`, `Stoch_n`, `ADX` with `DX = 100·|+DI − −DI| / max(ε, +DI + −DI)` and `ADX = Wilder_smooth(DX, n)`, `MFI_n`, `VWAP = Σ(TP·V) / max(ε, ΣV)` — **ε = 1e-9** on every denominator.

**Pipeline B L5 reference (v2):** PCA (sklearn, `n_components=8`, fit Train-only on standardized `X_raw`, persist `components_`/`mean_`/`explained_variance_` via joblib, transform = project); DWT (pywt `wavedec`, wavelet `db4`, `level=3`, rolling window `W=64`, causal, output = per-level energy); Autoencoder (MLP, enco
**Reference v2 parity checks (would extend the §1.1 chain, NOT in v1):**
[pipelineB_spec_eng.md](pipelineB_spec_eng.md)). Any `spec §N`, `register C-xx`, or `FLOW/L*.md`
